phrasalVerbs.definition: To reduce the amount of something you do, eat, or use.
examples: I'm trying to cut down on sugar.
He cut down on coffee last month.
We should cut down on plastic use.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To disconnect something (electricity/phone), or to remove something by cutting.
examples: They cut off our electricity by mistake.
The line was cut off during the call.
She cut off the damaged part of the plant.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To fasten buttons or a zip on clothes.
examples: Do up your coat—it's cold outside.
He did up his jacket before leaving.
Can you do up this zip for me?

phrasalVerbs.definition: To eat in a restaurant instead of at home.
examples: We usually eat out on Fridays.
Let's eat out tonight.
They ate out after the concert.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To trip and fall to the ground.
examples: Be careful—you might fall down.
He fell down and hurt his knee.
I nearly fell down on the wet floor.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To have an argument with someone and stop being friendly.
examples: I fell out with my best friend last year.
They fell out over a small misunderstanding.
Have you ever fallen out with a colleague?

phrasalVerbs.definition: To add information in the spaces on a form or document.
examples: Please fill in this form.
I filled in all the details online.
He forgot to fill in his phone number.
Fill up the bottle with water.
The room filled up quickly.
Can you fill up the car before we leave?
I need to find out what happened.
She found out the truth yesterday.
We'll find out the results tomorrow.

fillInExercises.sentence: I'm trying to ___ down ___ sweets this month.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Cut down on' means reduce the amount.

fillInExercises.sentence: The call was suddenly ___ off while we were talking.
We don't feel like cooking, so let's ___ out tonight.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Eat out' means dine at a restaurant.

fillInExercises.sentence: Be careful on that wet path, or you might ___.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Fall down' means to trip and fall.

fillInExercises.sentence: They ___ out with each other after a big argument.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Fall out with' means stop being friends after arguing.

fillInExercises.sentence: Please ___ ___ this application form before noon.
We'll ___ out who sent the anonymous email.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Could you [fill in|fill in] this online form for me?

lines.speaker: Joel
lines.text: Sure. But first I need to [find out|find out] your postcode.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: No problem. Also, can you [fill up|fill up] the kettle?

lines.speaker: Joel
lines.text: Done. By the way, we should [cut down on|cut down (on)] takeaway food.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: I agree—but maybe we can [eat out|eat out] this Saturday.

lines.speaker: Joel
lines.text: Deal. Just don't [fall down|fall down] the stairs while texting again!

lines.speaker: Paula
lines.text: I [fell out with|fall out (with)] my roommate this morning.

lines.speaker: Nico
lines.text: Oh no. Did you two [fill in|fill in] the complaint form afterwards?

lines.speaker: Paula
lines.text: A bit. Then my internet was [cut off|cut off] for an hour.

lines.speaker: Nico
lines.text: That's rough. Did you [find out|find out] why?

lines.speaker: Paula
lines.text: Yes, they needed me to [fill in|fill in] a missing payment form.

lines.speaker: Nico
lines.text: At least now you can [eat out|eat out] and relax a little tonight.
